coll: Quire structure only indicated for palimpsested folios (ff. 50 to 81): Quire 1: 1x6+1 (56), Quire 2: 1x6+1+1 (64), Quire 3: 1+1+1 (67), Quire 4: 1x4+1 (72), Quire 5: 1x6+1+1+1 (81)

note: Sloping pointed majuscule | Contrast between thick and thin strokes, ornamental elements at the end of thin strokes

note.value: Description by Pasquale Orsini

Bibliography: Most commonly quoted abbreviation for Triodion edition: TR = Τριῴδιον κατανυκτικόν, Rome 1879 ; For other abbreviations of editions see H. Follieri, Initia Hymnorum Ecclesiae Graecae, I-V.2, Studi e testi, 211-215bis (Vatican City, 1960-1966)
Description of undertext by Pasquale Orsini

Bibliography: Most commonly quoted abbreviation for Triodion edition: TR = Τριῴδιον κατανυκτικόν, Rome 1879 ; For other abbreviations of editions see H. Follieri, Initia Hymnorum Ecclesiae Graecae, I-V.2, Studi e testi, 211-215bis (Vatican City, 1960-1966)
Description by Pasquale Orsini
Bibliography: Abbreviations for editions are as follows: Pent = Πεντηκοστάριον, ed. P. Vitali (Rome, 1738) ; PeR = Πεντηκοστάριον χαρμόσυνον (Rome, 1883) ; PeV = Πεντηκοστάριον (Venice, 1875)
Reconstructed folio order, ff. 81v (right side), 81r (left side), 81r (right side), 81v (left side)

The current fol. 81 was a bifolio (recto 270 degrees, verso 90 degrees) and therefore it consists of two folios (1 = left portion of fol. 81r / right portion of fol. 81v; 2 = right portion of fol. 81r / left portion of fol. 81v [only the inner half is preserved])
Description of undertext by Pasquale Orsini
Bibliography: Abbreviations for editions are as follows: Pent = Πεντηκοστάριον, ed. P. Vitali (Rome, 1738) ; PeR = Πεντηκοστάριον χαρμόσυνον (Rome, 1883) ; PeV = Πεντηκοστάριον (Venice, 1875)

location.repository: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai

note.value: Ff. 50 to 81 are parchment and palimpsest, the rest are paper
Consecutive pencil foliation in large numbers at center top, followed here; parchment ff. 50-81 also carry small numbers in upper right (not followed)
Spine is leather on fabric. Fraying; front and back covers missing.
Bound quires
All single folios (except f. 81v) have stubs as their conjoins
